title: Plan a Transit Transfer
summary: Switch from one bus or train to another to reach a destination.
learnerPromise: You will be able to plan a trip that involves switching from one bus or train to another with confidence.
whyItMatters: Most transit trips require at least one transfer. Knowing how to plan one helps you travel farther and reach places that no single route serves directly.
realLifeUses: Taking a bus to a train station and switching to a train for the rest of your trip
Switching buses at a transit hub to reach a neighborhood on the other side of town
Planning a commute to school or work that involves two or more routes
Helping a friend figure out how to get to an event using multiple transit lines
materialsNeeded: Transit schedules or a trip-planning app
Paper and pen or a notes app
result: You have a written plan showing your first route, transfer point, second route, and estimated arrival time.
youWillLearn: How to identify the best transfer point between two routes
How to match arrival and departure times at a transfer stop
How to plan for walking between platforms or stops if needed
How to build a backup plan in case you miss your connection
miniChallenge: Plan a trip from a starting location to a destination that requires one transfer. Write down both route numbers, the transfer stop name, and the total estimated travel time.
steps: Look up the route from your starting point to a central transfer stop or hub.
Find the connecting route from that stop to your final destination.
Check the schedules to make sure the arrival and departure times line up reasonably.
Note the transfer stop name and any walking directions between platforms.
Write down both route numbers, the transfer stop, and your backup option if you miss the connection.
completionCriteria: You identified a valid transfer point where two routes meet
Your plan includes both route numbers and the transfer stop name
You estimated a realistic total travel time
commonProblems: The connecting bus or train leaves just before you arrive
Transfer stops can be on opposite sides of a large street or station
Routes sometimes run less frequently on weekends or evenings
tips: Allow at least five to ten minutes between connections when possible
Check if your transfer stop has a shelter or indoor waiting area
Have the next departure time ready in case you miss your first connection
